_note: A 4-turn headless Kubernetes incident-response agent loop. Stable system+tools prefix (cacheable), growing history, and a verbose volatile tool output each turn that carries one decision-driving line buried past char 120 in the noise. DECISION: markers are what the offline DeterministicRunner keys on, so ablation/certification have ground truth. Arc: triage CrashLoopBackOff -> diagnose root cause -> confirm rollback target -> execute rollback.

blocks.text:
You are an autonomous Kubernetes incident-response agent operating headless on call.
You triage deployment alerts, inspect pod state, and remediate within policy without a human in the loop.
Operating policy: prefer rollback to a known-good revision over hotfixing in prod.
DECISION: when pods are CrashLoopBackOff, inspect root cause before acting; prefer rollback_undo over in-place patch

AVAILABLE TOOLS (json schema, abbreviated):
- kubectl_get(resource, namespace, selector): returns JSON list of matching Kubernetes resources.
- describe_pod(namespace, pod): returns full pod description including events, status, and resource limits.
- rollout_history(namespace, deployment): returns revision history for a deployment with change-cause annotations.
- rollout_undo(namespace, deployment, to_revision): rolls the deployment back to the specified revision; returns confirmation JSON.
- get_metrics(service, window): returns time-series for cpu/mem/error-rate/latency as JSON.
DECISION: tool rollout_undo is available and is the preferred remediation for crash-looping pods caused by a bad deploy

kubectl_get("pods", "production", "app=checkout") -> {"apiVersion":"v1","items":[{"metadata":{"name":"checkout-7d9f8b6c4-xk2pq","namespace":"production"},"status":{"phase":"Running","containerStatuses":[{"name":"checkout","ready":true,"restartCount":0,"image":"acme/checkout:v1.6.0"}]}},{"metadata":{"name":"checkout-85c7d9f4b-lm3nz","namespace":"production"},"status":{"phase":"Running","containerStatuses":[{"name":"checkout","ready":false,"restartCount":7,"state":{"waiting":{"reason":"CrashLoopBackOff"}},"image":"acme/checkout:v1.7.0"}]}},{"metadata":{"name":"checkout-85c7d9f4b-pw8vt","namespace":"production"},"status":{"phase":"Running","containerStatuses":[{"name":"checkout","ready":false,"restartCount":5,"state":{"waiting":{"reason":"CrashLoopBackOff"}},"image":"acme/checkout:v1.7.0"}]}},{"metadata":{"name":"checkout-85c7d9f4b-rq1xs","namespace":"production"},"status":{"phase":"Running","containerStatuses":[{"name":"checkout","ready":false,"restartCount":6,"state":{"waiting":{"reason":"CrashLoopBackOff"}},"image":"acme/checkout:v1.7.0"}]}},{"metadata":{"name":"checkout-7d9f8b6c4-zt5wy","namespace":"production"},"status":{"phase":"Running","containerStatuses":[{"name":"checkout","ready":true,"restartCount":0,"image":"acme/checkout:v1.6.0"}]}}],"kind":"List"}
fetched 5 pods; 2 on old replicaset (v1.6.0, healthy), 3 on new replicaset (v1.7.0, crashing)
info: deployment checkout is in a partial rollout state; rollout paused by liveness probe failures
info: restart counts on v1.7.0 pods are 5, 6, 7 — escalating rapidly over the last 8 minutes
DECISION: 3/5 pods CrashLoopBackOff on checkout; new image v1.7.0 is the likely culprit, inspect pod before rolling back
info: v1.6.0 pods continue serving traffic; partial availability maintained at 2/5 capacity
info: error-rate on the checkout service has risen to 38% from baseline 0.2% since deploy began
info: PodDisruptionBudget allows a minimum of 2 healthy pods; current state is at the boundary
debug: kubectl query resolved in 61ms; server-version=1.29.3; cluster=prod-us-east-1
debug: list resourceVersion=48821934; generation=7 observedGeneration=7
debug: labels applied: app=checkout tier=frontend env=production

RETRIEVED (speculative context, similarity=0.69): 'Kubernetes Capacity Planning Handbook Q2 2026' — This document covers expected node counts, autoscaler tuning targets, and reserved instance commitments for the production cluster over the next two quarters. It includes cost-per-namespace breakdowns, vertical pod autoscaler recommendations, and a section on right-sizing CPU requests for the frontend tier. None of this information is relevant to diagnosing a CrashLoopBackOff during an active incident; it was surfaced by broad semantic overlap on the keyword 'checkout production pods'. Appendix E contains a pod template reference that is entirely unrelated to the crash in progress.
ALERT fired: CrashLoopBackOff on deployment=checkout namespace=production. Investigate and remediate per policy.

AVAILABLE TOOLS (json schema, abbreviated):
- kubectl_get(resource, namespace, selector): returns JSON list of matching Kubernetes resources.
- describe_pod(namespace, pod): returns full pod description including events, status, and resource limits.
- rollout_history(namespace, deployment): returns revision history for a deployment with change-cause annotations.
- rollout_undo(namespace, deployment, to_revision): rolls the deployment back to the specified revision; returns confirmation JSON.
- get_metrics(service, window): returns time-series for cpu/mem/error-rate/latency as JSON.
DECISION: tool rollout_undo is available and is the preferred remediation for crash-looping pods caused by a bad deploy
[turn 0 summary] Listed checkout pods; 3/5 in CrashLoopBackOff on new image v1.7.0, 2/5 healthy on v1.6.0. Error rate spiked to 38%. Next: describe a crashing pod to identify root cause.

describe_pod("production", "checkout-85c7d9f4b-lm3nz") -> {"kind":"Pod","metadata":{"name":"checkout-85c7d9f4b-lm3nz","namespace":"production","labels":{"app":"checkout","pod-template-hash":"85c7d9f4b"}},"spec":{"containers":[{"name":"checkout","image":"acme/checkout:v1.7.0","env":[{"name":"DATABASE_URL","valueFrom":{"secretKeyRef":{"name":"checkout-db","key":"url"}}},{"name":"STRIPE_API_KEY","valueFrom":{"secretKeyRef":{"name":"checkout-payments","key":"api_key"}}}],"resources":{"requests":{"cpu":"250m","memory":"512Mi"},"limits":{"cpu":"1","memory":"1Gi"}}}]},"status":{"phase":"Running","containerStatuses":[{"name":"checkout","restartCount":7,"lastState":{"terminated":{"exitCode":1,"reason":"Error","finishedAt":"2026-06-21T11:42:17Z"}}}]},"events":[{"type":"Warning","reason":"BackOff","message":"Back-off restarting failed container checkout in pod checkout-85c7d9f4b-lm3nz"},{"type":"Warning","reason":"Failed","message":"Error: failed to create containerd task: failed to create shim task: OCI runtime create failed"}]}
container exit code: 1; last termination reason: Error; container never passed readiness probe
log tail (last 5 lines before crash): FATAL: required environment variable PAYMENT_GATEWAY_URL is not set; startup aborted
log tail: panic: runtime error — missing required config; see PAYMENT_GATEWAY_URL in deployment spec
DECISION: new image v1.7.0 crashes on a missing env var PAYMENT_GATEWAY_URL, a regression introduced in the latest deploy; rollback is the correct remediation
log tail: goroutine 1 [running]: main.mustGetenv(0xc000112080, 0x13) — startup.go:47
info: secret checkout-payments exists and is mounted correctly; PAYMENT_GATEWAY_URL is a newly required var absent from the deployment manifest
info: the missing variable was added in the v1.7.0 codebase but not added to the Helm values; a config drift issue
info: pod has been restarted 7 times in 9 minutes; exponential back-off now at ~5 minute interval
debug: describe resolved in 74ms; pod uid=a3f1bc92-44e7-4d11-9c3b-7f082de61a04

RETRIEVED (speculative context, similarity=0.65): 'Helm Chart Authoring Best Practices — Internal Style Guide' — a 40-page guide covering chart versioning, values.yaml structure, templating conventions, and how to write reusable subcharts. Includes a section on environment variable injection via ConfigMaps versus Secrets. This document would be useful for a postmortem review of the config-drift root cause, but has no bearing on the immediate rollback decision. Pulled due to keyword overlap on 'deployment spec' and 'environment variable'. The agent does not cite this document.
Continue investigation. Stay within policy.

- kubectl_get(resource, namespace, selector): returns JSON list of matching Kubernetes resources.

- rollout_history(namespace, deployment): returns revision history for a deployment with change-cause annotations.

DECISION: tool rollout_undo is available and is the preferred remediation for crash-looping pods caused by a bad deploy
[turn 0 summary] Listed checkout pods; 3/5 in CrashLoopBackOff on new image v1.7.0, 2/5 healthy on v1.6.0. Error rate spiked to 38%. Next: describe a crashing pod to identify root cause.

[turn 1 summary] Described crashing pod; root cause is missing env var PAYMENT_GATEWAY_URL introduced in v1.7.0 but absent from Helm values — config drift. Exit code 1 on every startup. Rollback is the correct fix; need to identify the safe target revision.

rollout_history("production", "checkout") -> {"deployment":"checkout","namespace":"production","revisions":[{"revision":5,"changeCause":"deploy: checkout v1.5.2 — hotfix payment timeout","image":"acme/checkout:v1.5.2","deployedAt":"2026-05-14T08:22:00Z"},{"revision":6,"changeCause":"deploy: checkout v1.6.0 — feature/loyalty-points","image":"acme/checkout:v1.6.0","deployedAt":"2026-06-10T14:05:00Z","soakHours":263,"incidentCount":0},{"revision":7,"changeCause":"deploy: checkout v1.7.0 — feature/payment-gateway-v2","image":"acme/checkout:v1.7.0","deployedAt":"2026-06-21T11:33:00Z","soakHours":0.15,"incidentCount":1}],"currentRevision":7,"desiredRevision":7}
revision 5: acme/checkout:v1.5.2, deployed 2026-05-14, 38 soak-days, 0 incidents
revision 6: acme/checkout:v1.6.0, deployed 2026-06-10, 11 soak-days, 0 incidents — last known-good
revision 7: acme/checkout:v1.7.0, deployed 2026-06-21T11:33Z (9 minutes ago), 0 soak time, 1 active incident
DECISION: revision 7 is the bad deploy; roll back to revision 6 which has 11 days of soak time and zero incidents
info: revision 6 image acme/checkout:v1.6.0 is still present in the cluster image cache on all nodes
info: rollback to revision 6 will reuse the existing v1.6.0 replicaset (checkout-7d9f8b6c4) already partially healthy
info: estimated time to full recovery after rollback: 60-90 seconds based on readiness probe intervals
debug: rollout_history resolved in 38ms; cluster=prod-us-east-1; deployment generation=7

RETRIEVED (speculative context, similarity=0.62): 'Deployment Frequency and Change Failure Rate — DORA Metrics Dashboard Q2 2026' — a quarterly summary of deployment metrics across all services, including mean time to recovery, change failure rate by team, and deployment volume trends. The checkout service shows a CFR of 2.1% year-to-date, with this incident now contributing to the quarterly total. This document contains useful postmortem context but is entirely irrelevant to executing the rollback decision. The agent does not cite it.
Proceed with remediation. Confirm the rollback target before executing.

AVAILABLE TOOLS (json schema, abbreviated):
- kubectl_get(resource, namespace, selector): returns JSON list of matching Kubernetes resources.
- describe_pod(namespace, pod): returns full pod description including events, status, and resource limits.
- rollout_history(namespace, deployment): returns revision history for a deployment with change-cause annotations.
- rollout_undo(namespace, deployment, to_revision): rolls the deployment back to the specified revision; returns confirmation JSON.
- get_metrics(service, window): returns time-series for cpu/mem/error-rate/latency as JSON.
DECISION: tool rollout_undo is available and is the preferred remediation for crash-looping pods caused by a bad deploy
[turn 0 summary] Listed checkout pods; 3/5 in CrashLoopBackOff on new image v1.7.0, 2/5 healthy on v1.6.0. Error rate spiked to 38%. Next: describe a crashing pod to identify root cause.

[turn 1 summary] Described crashing pod; root cause is missing env var PAYMENT_GATEWAY_URL introduced in v1.7.0 but absent from Helm values — config drift. Exit code 1 on every startup. Rollback is the correct fix; need to identify the safe target revision.

[turn 2 summary] Pulled rollout history; revision 7 (v1.7.0) is the bad deploy with 9 minutes soak and 1 active incident. Revision 6 (v1.6.0) has 11 soak-days and zero incidents — confirmed safe rollback target. Executing rollback now.

rollout_undo("production", "checkout", 6) -> {"deployment":"checkout","namespace":"production","rolledBackTo":{"revision":6,"image":"acme/checkout:v1.6.0","changeCause":"deploy: checkout v1.6.0 — feature/loyalty-points"},"status":"progressing","startedAt":"2026-06-21T11:43:02Z","observedGeneration":8,"conditions":[{"type":"Progressing","status":"True","reason":"NewReplicaSetAvailable"},{"type":"Available","status":"True","reason":"MinimumReplicasAvailable"}]}
rollback initiated; deployment generation bumped to 8; target replicaset checkout-7d9f8b6c4 scaling up
post-check (t+30s): 5/5 pods ready on acme/checkout:v1.6.0; replicaset checkout-85c7d9f4b (v1.7.0) scaled to 0
post-check (t+30s): error rate returned to 0.2% (baseline); p99 latency 94ms (nominal)
DECISION: rolled back to revision 6, all 5 pods healthy on v1.6.0, incident resolved; file follow-up for missing PAYMENT_GATEWAY_URL in Helm values
post-check: PodDisruptionBudget constraints satisfied throughout rollback; zero pods below minimum at any point
info: incident duration 11 minutes from alert to full recovery; no data loss, no payment failures logged
info: follow-up ticket RB-4421 auto-filed: add PAYMENT_GATEWAY_URL to checkout Helm values before re-deploying v1.7.0
info: notifying on-call channel #incidents with resolution summary
debug: rollout_undo resolved in 112ms; audit_id=rollback-8a3f; signed_by=policy-engine; cluster=prod-us-east-1

RETRIEVED (speculative context, similarity=0.61): 'Incident Postmortem Template and Blameless Culture Guide' — an internal template for writing postmortem documents after production incidents, covering the five-whys method, timeline reconstruction, contributing factors, and action items. Useful for the follow-up postmortem session, but completely irrelevant to the live rollback execution happening in this turn.
